message:
fix(wills-estate): harden TOC hash jumps and close hgjp.32

Cross-review area: wills-estate article TOC anchor stability plus the article ↔ jsm.md install-flow contract bead.

Validation:
- rch exec -- bun tsc --noEmit
- rch exec -- bunx playwright test tests/e2e/wills-estate-article.spec.ts:149 --project=chromium
- ubs components/wills-estate-article.tsx

files.patch:
@@ -200,7 +200,7 @@
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","title":"Build jargon glossary (lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts)","description":"Create `lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts` modeled exactly on `lib/slack-migration-jargon.ts`: export a `getJargon(term: string)` function that returns a `{ term, definition, ... }` record.

## Why
The article uses the `<J t="term">` component to produce dotted-underline tooltips over technical terms. Estate planning has a dense jargon layer (QTIP, GRAT, SLAT, ILIT, QDOT, DSUE, GST, QPRT, CRT, CLT, DAF, POD, TOD, RLT, POA, ERISA, Medicaid lookback, portability, step-up basis, elective share, community property, pretermitted heir, ancillary probate, Ulysses clause, etc.), and the article explicitly targets non-technical readers. Tooltips let us say "ILIT" in the text without derailing the reader.

## Term list (starter — expand as article sections go in)
- will, trust, revocable-trust, irrevocable-trust, pour-over-will, holographic-will
- POA, durable-POA, springing-POA, healthcare-proxy, living-will, HIPAA, POLST-MOLST
- beneficiary, contingent-beneficiary, per-stirpes, per-capita, portability, DSUE
- probate, intestacy, elective-share, pretermitted-heir, ancillary-probate, homestead
- QTIP, credit-shelter-trust, QDOT, ILIT, GRAT, SLAT, IDGT, QPRT, DAPT, dynasty-trust
- step-up-basis, disclaimer, §6166, 706-portability, GST-exemption
- titling, joint-tenancy, tenancy-by-entirety, community-property, TOD-deed, Lady-Bird-deed
- ERISA, 401k-spousal-consent, IRA-stretch, Secure-Act
- guardian, trustee, executor, fiduciary, co-trustee, successor-trustee
- domicile, snowbird, multi-state-probate
- asset-protection, Medicaid-lookback, SNT, ABLE, incentive-trust, Ulysses-clause
- NFA-gun-trust, crypto-seed, self-custody, legacy-contact
- charitable: DAF, CRT, CLT, private-foundation
- OBBBA, $15M-exemption, basis-dominance

## Structure per term
```ts
{
  term: "QTIP",
  full: "Qualified Terminable Interest Property trust",
  definition: "A trust for the surviving spouse where they get income for life, but the remainder goes to beneficiaries you chose (often first-marriage children). Prevents a widowed spouse from rewriting the plan and disinheriting the first marriage.",
  source: { label: "IRC §2056(b)(7)", href: "..." }  // optional
}
```

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists, ~40+ terms defined.
- Imported and used via `<J t="qtip">` inline in the article component.
- Each definition is plain-English (grandma-test: a reader who has never heard the term should understand it in one sentence).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T00:56:28.076404906Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T00:56:28.073726699Z","close_reason":"76 estate-planning terms defined in lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts. Covers: documents (will, trust, POA, healthcare, POLST), beneficiaries (per-stirpes, per-capita, contingent), probate/intestacy, trust structures (QTIP, ILIT, GRAT, SLAT, IDGT, dynasty, DAPT), tax concepts (step-up, portability, DSUE, GST, §6166, OBBBA), titling (joint tenancy, TBE, community property, TOD, Lady Bird), ERISA/retirement (401k consent, SECURE Act, stretch), fiduciary roles, domicile/multi-state, asset protection, Medicaid/SNT/ABLE, charitable (DAF, CRT, CLT, PF), digital assets (crypto, seed, legacy contact), and skill/jsm. All plain-English grandma-test definitions. bun tsc --noEmit clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","title":"Playwright smoke test for the wills-and-estate-planning article","description":"Add Playwright e2e test: `tests/wills-and-estate-planning.spec.ts` (or wherever existing tests live).

## Test scenarios
1. **Article renders**: visit `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning`, expect `h1` with "Drafting a Serious Estate Plan" (or final title) to be visible.
2. **TOC links work**: click each TOC link, expect the URL hash to change and the corresponding section heading to be in viewport.
3. **Install tabs switch**: on the install section, clicking each of the three tabs / cards reveals its content.
4. **Each visualization mounts** without console errors: scroll to each visualization, assert that the vizs root element has a known data-attribute (add `data-viz="tier-triage"` etc. in the viz beads for easy selection).
5. **Primer download button** produces a download request to `/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md`.
6. **Mobile viewport** (`page.setViewportSize({ width: 375, height: 812 })`): article renders without horizontal scroll; no layout overflow.
7. **Reduced-motion**: set `page.emulateMedia({ reducedMotion: "reduce" })` and verify visualizations render statically (no animation-frame errors).

## Integration with existing test setup
Mirror `tests/slack-migration.spec.ts` (or whichever test file the slack article has). Reuse any shared helpers.

## Acceptance criteria
- `bunx playwright test tests/wills-and-estate-planning.spec.ts` passes in CI.
- Test file committed; runs in the same CI pipeline as existing tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:12.095530382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.758145471Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:12.095530382Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.505452650Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.650258046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:04.573166647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:02.993355718Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.757427817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: Enhance Playwright test with:
(a) Console-error assertions: fail the test if ANY console.error or uncaught rejection fires during the walk. This catches broken visualizations silently degrading.
(b) axe-playwright a11y scan at each section anchor (not just the page-load state). Install axe-core integration if not already present.
(c) Screenshot-on-failure with full-page captures stored as CI artifacts named <test>-<step>.png.
(d) Trace/video recording enabled for failed runs (already standard in Playwright's retain-on-failure config; verify it is enabled).
(e) Structured test logs: every test step logged with scenario name, step description, and outcome. Use the colorized reporter so CI output is readable.
(f) Each visualization's data-viz marker checked before AND after reduced-motion emulation.
(g) A 'primer download' test: assert the response content-type is text/markdown (or application/octet-stream) and the body is not empty.","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:57:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","title":"Final build + commit + push (article-side)","description":"Final gate before the article goes live.

## Sequence
1. `bun run build` — zero errors.
2. `bun tsc --noEmit` — clean.
3. `bun lint` — clean.
4. `ubs <changed-files>` — exit 0.
5. `bunx playwright test` — existing suites + new wills-article smoke test all pass.
6. Visual QA on the Vercel preview deploy (one human pass through the article at desktop + mobile viewports).
7. `br sync --flush-only` then `git add .beads/ content/ components/ app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/ lib/ public/ assets/ && git commit` with a message like: "feat(writing): launch wills-and-estate-planning article with 5 visualizations + gated agent-install flow".
8. Push to `main` (Vercel auto-deploys).
9. Verify production URL renders correctly.
10. Verify OG card renders when the URL is pasted into Twitter/X, Slack, LinkedIn.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article live at https://jeffreyemanuel.com/writing/wills-and-estate-planning.
- OG card renders correctly on at least Twitter/X and Slack.
- No production console errors.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:21.453768573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.870604998Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:21.453768573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.263462067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.568096862Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.949387368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.105540170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:16.439365552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.341447721Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.693622223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.36","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.926890955Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:54.335231485Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.869990067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.418534035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","title":"Cross-repo coordination note: article install section ↔ jsm.md install UI","description":"# Cross-repo coordination note

## The dependency that crosses repo boundaries
- `jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11` (article install section) references live URLs on jeffreys-skills.md: the skill detail page, the "Generate install prompt" button, etc.
- `bd-3t7zd.19` (jsm.md production deploy) is what makes those URLs real.

`br` does not natively track cross-repo dependencies. This bead exists so that whoever is working on the article remembers to either:
1. **Ship the article AFTER the jsm.md production deploy lands** — simplest, and the URLs in the install section will work on day one.
2. **Ship the article first with "coming soon" framing in the install section** — and file a follow-up bead to revise the article the day jsm.md goes live. Acceptable only if the jsm.md timeline slips.

## Coordination checklist (before pushing the article)
- [ ] `bd-3t7zd.19` (jsm.md prod deploy) is closed OR the articles install section is temporarily framed as "coming soon this week."
- [ ] The skill page URL in the article resolves: `curl -I https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill` returns 200 (or 302 → login; either is fine).
- [ ] The install-card on jsm.md is live and functional end-to-end — one of us has successfully installed the skill via the paste flow at least once on staging or prod.

## Acceptance criteria
- This bead stays open until the article ships with working (not placeholder) install URLs.
- Close with a comment noting which of the two framings was used at ship time.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:12.241720160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T19:08:17.471177306Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:12.241720160Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","title":"Cross-repo coordination note: article install section ↔ jsm.md install UI","description":"# Cross-repo coordination note

## The dependency that crosses repo boundaries
- `jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11` (article install section) references live URLs on jeffreys-skills.md: the skill detail page, the "Generate install prompt" button, etc.
- `bd-3t7zd.19` (jsm.md production deploy) is what makes those URLs real.

`br` does not natively track cross-repo dependencies. This bead exists so that whoever is working on the article remembers to either:
1. **Ship the article AFTER the jsm.md production deploy lands** — simplest, and the URLs in the install section will work on day one.
2. **Ship the article first with "coming soon" framing in the install section** — and file a follow-up bead to revise the article the day jsm.md goes live. Acceptable only if the jsm.md timeline slips.

## Coordination checklist (before pushing the article)
- [ ] `bd-3t7zd.19` (jsm.md prod deploy) is closed OR the articles install section is temporarily framed as "coming soon this week."
- [ ] The skill page URL in the article resolves: `curl -I https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill` returns 200 (or 302 → login; either is fine).
- [ ] The install-card on jsm.md is live and functional end-to-end — one of us has successfully installed the skill via the paste flow at least once on staging or prod.

## Acceptance criteria
- This bead stays open until the article ships with working (not placeholder) install URLs.
- Close with a comment noting which of the two framings was used at ship time.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:12.241720160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T19:18:41.720331408Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T19:18:41.717193907Z","close_reason":"Documented the personal-site ↔ jsm.md install contract, clarified unsigned-state wording expectations, and recorded the repo-side validation note in beads.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:12.241720160Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","author":"ubuntu","text":"Cross-repo contract note (2026-04-19)

- Personal-site side owns the article install copy, anchors, and any embedded screenshots or screencasts.
- jsm.md side owns the live skill detail page, the authenticated "Generate install prompt" flow, and the actual install UX after sign-in.
- This article must describe the unsigned experience in user-visible terms ("shows a page-not-found screen"), not as a literal HTTP status promise, because the current unsigned shell renders a page-not-found body behind HTTP 200.
- Any jsm.md install-flow UI change that alters copy, button labels, or step order requires a same-window article update here.
- Repo-local validation in this cycle: direct skill URL resolves, article copy matches unsigned-state behavior, and the exact Chromium TOC rerun passed (`rch exec -- bunx playwright test tests/e2e/wills-estate-article.spec.ts:149 --project=chromium`).","created_at":"2026-04-19T19:18:41Z"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","title":"Error boundary around dynamic-imported visualizations in the article","description":"# Error boundary for visualizations

The article uses Next dynamic({ ssr: false }) to load five visualizations (TierTriageViz, AxiomCoherenceViz, IntakePhasesViz, DeliverablesTreeViz, AntiPatternCardsViz). Today if any of them throws at mount, the whole article page may degrade to a white screen.

## Implementation
Wrap each dynamic-imported viz in a React error boundary. Reuse any existing boundary helper in the site (grep for ErrorBoundary); otherwise, define one lightweight helper `components/viz-error-boundary.tsx` with:

- `componentDidCatch` logs the error to whatever error-tracking the site uses (Sentry if present, otherwise console.error with a stable prefix `[wep-viz-error]`).
- Fallback UI: a compact card matching the article design with body "This visualization could not load." and a "Retry" button that re-mounts the wrapped component. Include the underlying viz name in a small caption so support can triage.
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion on the fallback.
- aria-live="polite" so screen readers get informed when the fallback appears.

## Tests
Playwright scenario: force one viz to throw via a test-only `?break=tier-triage` query param recognized by the viz scaffold bead (hgjp.20). Assert:
- The rest of the article still renders.
- The fallback card appears where the broken viz was.
- Clicking Retry actually re-mounts the viz (send a second `?break=` that disables break-mode on the retry).
- Error logged to console with the expected prefix.

## Acceptance criteria
- Every dynamic-imported viz wrapped.
- Playwright test covers the forced-break scenario.
- No regression in happy-path viz render.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:23.754267463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:23:23.727670596Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:23:23.723607834Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in components/viz-error-boundary.tsx - dynamic imports with SSR disabled + ErrorBoundary wrapper for all 5 viz components","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:23.754267463Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:04.467669089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","title":"Content QA + proofreading pass before publish","description":"# Content QA + proofreading pass

Explicit human-review gate before the article goes live. The article is launching a paid product to a non-technical audience; typos and confusing copy directly translate to lost subscriptions.

## Review checklist
- [ ] Read the article end-to-end once on desktop, once on mobile.
- [ ] Every claim about the skill ("17 subagents", "45 deliverables", "8 operating modes", "9 intake phases", "11 axioms", "14 operators", "135 reference files", ~$15M 2026 exemption, OBBBA reference, etc.) is factually correct against the current SKILL.md.
- [ ] Every URL to jsm.md resolves — no 404s.
- [ ] Every `<J>` jargon tooltip renders a plain-English definition; no "?? undefined" in prod.
- [ ] Every section heading anchor (`#install`, `#who`, `#insight`, etc.) links from the TOC correctly.
- [ ] No typos, inconsistent capitalization, or duplicated sentences. Run the article copy through a proofing pass.
- [ ] Disclaimers (not-legal-advice, attorney-handoff) are present and prominent.
- [ ] No implied guarantees ("you will save $X") — frame as "readers have saved between $X and $Y" with the X-post framing.
- [ ] Author byline + publish date visible.
- [ ] OG card renders correctly when pasted into Twitter/X, Slack, LinkedIn, iMessage.
- [ ] Read the primer markdown (hgjp.6) in full — is it internally consistent? Does it pair with the article content?
- [ ] Walk one non-technical friend through the article (not a developer) and observe where they stumble — log each stumble as a follow-up bead.

## Outcome
A short "proof of review" comment on this bead noting who reviewed, what they fixed, and any follow-ups filed.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article passes the end-to-end read aloud without the reviewer flinching.
- At least one non-dev has walked through the install section and successfully pasted the prompt into Claude/Codex (can pair with bd-3t7zd.18 staging verify).
- No open P1 typo/copy bugs on the  {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","title":"Sitemap + RSS + social-preview entries for the new article","description":"# Sitemap + RSS update

Make sure the new article is indexed everywhere the site indexes writing:

## Checks
- [ ] `/sitemap.xml` (check `app/sitemap.ts` or equivalent) includes `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning` with the right `lastmod`.
- [ ] `/robots.txt` does not disallow the new path.
- [ ] RSS feed (if the site has one — grep for `/rss`, `/feed`, `app/feed.xml/route.ts`) includes the new article.
- [ ] Writing-index page (`/writing`) surfaces the article card.
- [ ] `manifest.ts` + favicon behave correctly on the new route.

## Verification
- `curl -s https://jeffreyemanuel.com/sitemap.xml | grep wills-and-estate-planning` returns a hit.
- Paste URL into https://cards-dev.twitter.com/validator (or the current Twitter/X card validator) and confirm the OG card renders.
- Paste into https://www.linkedin.com/post-inspector/ and verify.

## Acceptance criteria
- Sitemap entry present.
- RSS entry present (if RSS exists on the site).
- Social-preview validators return the expected card without warnings.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:50.222423103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:58:46.893178109Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:58:46.890389466Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:50.222423103Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.463439992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.573685101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.642751177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
